name: Eurovision
description: Europe's annual song contest — winners, hosts, and iconic moments

questions.q: In what year was the first Eurovision Song Contest held?

source.notes: First held in Lugano, Switzerland. Seven countries competed. Switzerland won.

questions.q: Which iconic Swedish pop group won Eurovision 1974 with 'Waterloo'?

source.notes: Launched ABBA's global career. Often voted the greatest Eurovision song of all time.

questions.q: Which Irish singer won Eurovision a record three times (as soloist)?

source.notes: Won 1980 and 1987 as performer. Won 1992 as songwriter. Only person to win 3 times.

questions.q: Which country has won Eurovision the most times (7 wins)?

source.notes: Ireland's 7 wins, most recent 1996. Sweden tied at 7 after their 2023 win.

questions.q: Which heavy metal/rock band famously won Eurovision 2006 for Finland in monster costumes?

source.notes: Song: 'Hard Rock Hallelujah.' Finland's first Eurovision win.

questions.q: Which Swedish singer won Eurovision 2012 with 'Euphoria'?

source.notes: Won again in 2023 with 'Tattoo.' Only second person (after Johnny Logan) to win twice as a performer.

questions.q: Which British performer won Eurovision in 1967 with 'Puppet on a String'?

source.notes: UK's first Eurovision win. Shaw famously performed barefoot.

questions.q: Who is the long-time UK Eurovision commentator on BBC, known for his witty commentary?

source.notes: Took over from Terry Wogan in 2009. Irish broadcaster.

questions.q: In which country was Eurovision 2023 held (after Ukraine's scheduled hosting was disrupted by war)?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: year fixed from 2022 to 2023 — held in Liverpool, UK on behalf of Ukraine after Russia's invasion.

questions.q: Which Norwegian violinist-singer won Eurovision 2009 with 'Fairytale'?

source.notes: Record-breaking 387 points at the time. Born in Belarus, Norwegian citizen.

questions.q: The 'Big Five' Eurovision countries — automatic qualifiers to the final — include the UK, France, Germany, Spain, and who?

source.notes: Five biggest financial contributors. Italy was added in 2011 (previously Big Four).

questions.q: Which Israeli singer won Eurovision 2018 with the song 'Toy'?

source.notes: Netta Barzilai. Anti-harassment anthem with 'chicken noises.' Netta won before Dana in 1998.

questions.q: Which Austrian bearded drag queen won Eurovision 2014 with 'Rise Like a Phoenix'?

source.notes: Stage name of Thomas Neuwirth. Austria's first win since 1966.

questions.q: Which country has the most Eurovision Song Contest appearances overall?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: rephrased — no country participated in literally every year (Germany missed 1996). Germany has the most overall appearances.

questions.q: What is the maximum number of points any country can receive from another country's combined jury + televote in modern Eurovision?

source.notes: Since 2016, juries and televotes are counted separately. Each awards up to 12 points. Total: 24.

questions.q: Which country abandoned Eurovision in the 1970s after giving controversial 'nul points'?

source.notes: Austria withdrew 1975-76. 'Nul points' scandals have happened to many countries (Norway most famously 4 times).

questions.q: Who commentates Eurovision for the US as part of its NBC/Peacock broadcast?

source.notes: Tan France (Queer Eye) commentates with Rita Ora. Eurovision has aired in US since 2016.

questions.q: The 'Jury Points' and 'Televote' were combined into one score until what year, when they were split?

source.notes: Since 2016, jury and public vote announced separately, creating more dramatic reveals.

questions.q: What is the iconic Eurovision host greeting traditionally given at the start of the show?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: fixed correct answer to 'Good evening, Europe' — the iconic Eurovision host greeting.

questions.q: What was Celine Dion's winning Eurovision country in 1988?

source.notes: Won with 'Ne partez pas sans moi.' Dion (Canadian) represented Switzerland. One point margin of victory!
